query:
INSERT INTO questions (questionText, modelAnswer, shortAnswer, acceptedVariations, difficulty, stageOrder, category, hint) VALUES
-- EASY (7 questions)
('What is a primary key in a database?', 'A primary key is a unique identifier for each record in a database table. It ensures that no two rows have the same value and cannot be NULL.', 'primary key', '["pk","primary","unique identifier","unique key"]', 'easy', 1, 'Fundamentals', 'Think about what uniquely identifies each row in a table.'),
('What does SQL stand for?', 'SQL stands for Structured Query Language. It is the standard language used to communicate with relational databases.', 'structured query language', '["sql","structured query lang","query language"]', 'easy', 2, 'SQL Basics', 'It is a language used to query and manipulate data in relational databases.'),
('What is a foreign key?', 'A foreign key is a column or set of columns in one table that references the primary key of another table, establishing a relationship between the two tables.', 'foreign key', '["fk","reference key","referencing key"]', 'easy', 3, 'Fundamentals', 'It links two tables together by referencing a key in another table.'),
('What is normalization in databases?', 'Normalization is the process of organizing a database to reduce data redundancy and improve data integrity by dividing large tables into smaller related tables.', 'normalization', '["normalisation","normal form","data normalization"]', 'easy', 4, 'Database Design', 'It is a process to reduce redundancy and organize data efficiently.'),
('What is a database index?', 'A database index is a data structure that improves the speed of data retrieval operations on a database table at the cost of additional storage space and slower write operations.', 'index', '["db index","database index","indexing"]', 'easy', 5, 'Performance', 'Think of it like the index at the back of a book that helps you find information faster.'),
('What is a NULL value in SQL?', 'NULL in SQL represents the absence of a value or an unknown value. It is not the same as zero or an empty string.', 'null', '["null value","missing value","unknown value","absence of value"]', 'easy', 6, 'SQL Basics', 'It represents the absence of any value, not zero or empty.'),
('What is a relational database?', 'A relational database is a type of database that stores and provides access to data points that are related to one another, organized into tables with rows and columns.', 'relational database', '["rdbms","relational db","relational model"]', 'easy', 7, 'Fundamentals', 'It organizes data into tables with relationships between them.'),

-- MEDIUM (7 questions)
('What is a database transaction?', 'A database transaction is a sequence of one or more SQL operations treated as a single unit of work. Transactions follow ACID properties: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability.', 'transaction', '["db transaction","atomic transaction","acid transaction"]', 'medium', 1, 'Transactions', 'Think about a bank transfer — all steps must succeed or none should.'),
('What does ACID stand for in database systems?', 'ACID stands for Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability. These are the four key properties that guarantee reliable processing of database transactions.', 'acid', '["atomicity consistency isolation durability","acid properties","acid compliance"]', 'medium', 2, 'Transactions', 'These are four properties that ensure reliable database transactions.'),
('What is a JOIN in SQL?', 'A JOIN clause in SQL is used to combine rows from two or more tables based on a related column between them. Common types include INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN, and FULL JOIN.', 'join', '["sql join","table join","inner join"]', 'medium', 3, 'SQL Basics', 'It combines rows from multiple tables based on a matching column.'),
('What is database denormalization?', 'Denormalization is the process of intentionally introducing redundancy into a database by merging tables or adding redundant data to improve read performance, at the cost of increased storage and potential update anomalies.', 'denormalization', '["denormalisation","de-normalization","denormalize"]', 'medium', 4, 'Database Design', 'It is the opposite of normalization — adding redundancy to improve performance.'),
('What is a stored procedure?', 'A stored procedure is a prepared SQL code that can be saved and reused. It is a set of SQL statements stored in the database that can be called by name and executed with parameters.', 'stored procedure', '["procedure","sproc","stored proc"]', 'medium', 5, 'SQL Basics', 'It is a reusable block of SQL code stored in the database.'),
('What is a database view?', 'A database view is a virtual table based on the result set of a SQL query. It contains rows and columns just like a real table but does not store data physically; it is derived from one or more tables.', 'view', '["virtual table","db view","sql view"]', 'medium', 6, 'Database Design', 'It is like a saved SQL query that acts as a virtual table.'),
('What is the difference between DELETE and TRUNCATE in SQL?', 'DELETE removes rows one at a time and logs each deletion, can use WHERE clause, and can be rolled back. TRUNCATE removes all rows at once, is faster, cannot use WHERE clause, and typically cannot be rolled back.', 'truncate', '["delete vs truncate","truncate delete difference"]', 'medium', 7, 'SQL Basics', 'One removes specific rows and is logged; the other removes all rows at once.'),

-- HARD (7 questions)
('What is a deadlock in database systems?', 'A deadlock occurs when two or more transactions are waiting for each other to release locks, creating a circular dependency where none of the transactions can proceed. Database systems detect and resolve deadlocks by aborting one of the transactions.', 'deadlock', '["dead lock","circular wait","deadlock situation"]', 'hard', 1, 'Concurrency', 'It is a situation where two transactions are permanently blocked waiting for each other.'),
('What is the CAP theorem?', 'The CAP theorem states that a distributed database system can only guarantee two of the three properties simultaneously: Consistency (all nodes see the same data), Availability (every request receives a response), and Partition tolerance (system continues despite network failures).', 'cap theorem', '["cap","brewer theorem","consistency availability partition"]', 'hard', 2, 'Distributed Systems', 'It describes trade-offs in distributed systems between three key properties.'),
('What is a B-tree index?', 'A B-tree (Balanced Tree) index is a self-balancing tree data structure that maintains sorted data and allows searches, sequential access, insertions, and deletions in O(log n) time. It is the most common index structure used in relational databases.', 'b-tree', '["btree","balanced tree","b tree index"]', 'hard', 3, 'Performance', 'It is a balanced tree data structure used for efficient data retrieval in databases.'),
('What is MVCC in database systems?', 'MVCC (Multi-Version Concurrency Control) is a concurrency control method that keeps multiple versions of data to allow readers and writers to operate concurrently without blocking each other. Each transaction sees a snapshot of the database at a point in time.', 'mvcc', '["multi version concurrency control","multiversion concurrency","multi-version concurrency"]', 'hard', 4, 'Concurrency', 'It allows multiple transactions to read and write simultaneously by keeping multiple data versions.'),
('What is database sharding?', 'Database sharding is a horizontal scaling technique that partitions data across multiple database instances (shards), where each shard holds a subset of the data. It improves performance and scalability for large datasets.', 'sharding', '["horizontal partitioning","data sharding","db sharding"]', 'hard', 5, 'Distributed Systems', 'It is a way to split a large database across multiple machines horizontally.'),
('What is the difference between OLTP and OLAP?', 'OLTP (Online Transaction Processing) is optimized for fast, frequent transactional operations like inserts and updates in operational systems. OLAP (Online Analytical Processing) is optimized for complex queries and analysis on large historical datasets in data warehouses.', 'oltp olap', '["online transaction processing","online analytical processing","oltp vs olap"]', 'hard', 6, 'Database Design', 'One handles day-to-day transactions; the other handles analytical queries on large datasets.'),
('What is a query execution plan?', 'A query execution plan (or query plan) is a sequence of operations that the database engine uses to execute a SQL query. It shows how the database will access data, which indexes will be used, and the order of operations. EXPLAIN command is used to view it.', 'execution plan', '["query plan","explain plan","query execution plan"]', 'hard', 7, 'Performance', 'It shows the step-by-step strategy the database uses to execute your SQL query.');
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway03.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 4HH9rPrcVNL816g.6517669b2b3e --database bPFm9h3JEmLnjyZUQkTYo7 --execute INSERT INTO questions (questionText, modelAnswer, shortAnswer, acceptedVariations, difficulty, stageOrder, category, hint) VALUES
